reasons: Based on a visual assessment of the provided images, this dog appears to have a Body Condition Score of approximately 7 (Heavy) on the WSAVA 9-point scale. Viewing the dog from above (image 1), there is no discernible waist behind the rib cage; the dog's back appears broad, and the body outline is relatively straight from the ribs to the hips. From the side view (image 2), an abdominal tuck is absent; the underline of the belly is flat or slightly sagging rather than sloping upwards toward the hind legs. The dog has an overall thick and blocky appearance, suggesting a significant layer of fat covering the ribs, lumbar area, and abdomen. Note that a definitive BCS requires physical palpation to assess the fat cover over the ribs and spine.
